title: MWP5 Final Project: 5 findability opportunities to access the portfolio dataset

steps: Use the DOI to directly access the dataset. A DOI is a Digital Object Identifier. That is a Digital Identifier of an Object, not an Identifier of a Digital Object. DOIs allow objects to be identified and accessed with certainty. Once DOIs are allocated, they never change for that object (Driven by DOI, n.d.). Driven by DOI . (n.d.). Retrieved March 30, 2022, from https://www.doi.org/driven_by_DOI.html
Searching via PubMed Central with the keywords “ pubmed literature model ” you will hit the data descriptor article as the first result.

Use figshare repository where the data is located to find the dataset. For that, open the repository website and type or paste the keywords pubmed and literature which were selected from the dataset title STS Model of the PubMed Literature . The results can be filtered and sorted to reduce the amount of found datasets and to find a proper match, e.g. from 7109 to 2437 datasets here. Tip: use a "+" sign to reduce the number of found results as it serves the purpose of exact matching.

For that, open the DOI system on doi.org , add "/" sign and the unique identifier of the dataset (doi: 10.6084/m9.figshare.12743639.v1) or the data descriptor article (doi: 10.1038/s41597-020-00749-y) as shown in the video. You will be navigated to the requested source at once.
Another opportunity to find the dataset in the DOI system is to use the search field by typing in or pasting the identifier as shown in the video.

Use Google dataset search which collects a lot of well-curated datasets' metadata if they conform to the standards defined by the schema.org consortium. For that, open the Google dataset search engine and type or paste the keywords pubmed and literature . When you type, it will show a dropdown with the best match results that you can open right away as it is shown in the video below. If you press the "enter" key it will show a page with search results that can be filtered to find a proper dataset.

Use Dimensions Abstracting & Indexing database where the datasets from figshare are harvested automatically. From the home page access right away the web retrieval system . Use the keywords pubmed literature in the search bar. Then the results can be filtered by multiple parameters, e.g. type of data, repository name, year, etc. Here the repository name filter was applied - the number was reduced from 2732 to 30 results. The third hit is the wanted dataset. If searching the keywords " pubmed literature model " the hit will appear already on the first page (6th here).
creator: Anastasiia Iarkaeva
